dir: Site is north of Eckerman Corners (intersection of M-28 and M-123).  Drive north on M-123 towards Paradise for about 1.3 miles to the railroad grade heading east.  The railroad grade goes directly to the river but it is a tight fit for many vehicles.
sd: The brook trout population is self-sustaining with good natural reproduction throughout the system.  Surveys have found fish upwards of 14 inches with average lengths above 10 inches.
desig: The East Branch of the Tahquamenon River in this location is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: East Branch Two Hearted River County Road 414 Bridge
d: While fishing this river anglers can find deep holes and plenty of overhead cover for big trout.  The most successful anglers will use natural bait.  Wading is possible but most anglers will fish the river from the bank.  The river meanders through stands of mature red and white pine trees making ta
dir: This site is north of the Village of Newberry.  Drive north on M-123 to Four Mile Corner (County Road 407) and make a left (West).  Follow County Road (CR) 407 for about 15 miles to CR 414 and make a right (East).  Follow CR 414 to the intersection w
sd: A good number of wild brook trout exist in this river.  Anglers report good catches of fish frequently reaching 15 inches.  When steelhead are in the Two Hearted River, some will hit the East Branch.
desig: The East Branch of the Two Hearted River at this location is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide for

n: Little Two Hearted River at County Road 412
d: This stream is very wadeable in this area.  Log jams are very numerous and offer excellent cover for brook trout.  Anglers may use spinners or natural bait this far down in the system.  Floating is not recommended due to various log jams that make this river impassable.
dir: This location is north of the village of Newberry.  Drive north on M-123 for about 18 miles to County Road (CR) 500 and turn left.  Follow CR 500 for about 11.5 miles to CR 412 and turn left (West).  Follow CR 412 for about 2 miles and you will find
sd: Wild brook trout are present here in moderate numbers.  Anglers report catches of larger brook trout in this stream reach.  Surveys typically find fish ranging in size from 7 to 14 inches but larger fish have been reported by anglers upwards of 17 in
desig: The Little Two Hearted River at County Road 412 is a designated type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: Little Two Hearted River Fisher Bridge
d: This stream can be a challenge to fish but those who do fish here have good results.  Expect plenty of overhanging tag alder along the majority of the banks.  Wading is the best approach for anglers.  Most anglers will use natural bait on this type of stream hitting the deep holes in the bends and l
dir: This site is located north of the village of Newberry.  Drive north on M-123 for about 18 miles to County Road (CR) 500 and turn left.  Follow CR 500 for about 7 miles to CR 501 (Fisher Bridge Road) and turn left (South).  Once on CR 501, Fisher Brid
sd: A good number of wild brook trout are present here.  Anglers report catches of larger brook brout in this stream reach.  Surveys typically find fish ranging in size from 7 to 12 inches but larger fish have been reported by anglers upwards of 17 inche
desig: This section of the Little Tow Hearted River is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

d: This lake is created by a large beaver dam holding back groundwater coming out of the hillsides.  There is a primary channel that flows through the center of the lake so anglers should target that along with any open pockets in the scattered vegetation.  The access site limits anglers to small hand-
dir: This location is North of Eckerman Corners (M-28 and M-123).  Driver North on M-123 for about 11 miles to Lakeshore Drive (Curley Lewis Highway) and turn right (East).  Follow Lakeshore Drive for about two miles to East West road (gravel road) and tu
sd: Fall fingerling brook trout are stocked annually.  Fisheries surveys have found good survival and growth of these stocked fish.  Brook trout typically range in size from 5 to 13 inches.
desig: Noamikong Lake is a designated Type A trout lake.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Lake Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

d: Naomikong Pond is a walk-in fishery with a gate at the parking lot.  Anglers wanting to float and fish can haul a hand-carry watercraft easily down the trail to the pond.  Shoreline angling access is plentiful with many locations to set up and fish.  Anglers should use artificial lures that mimic go
dir: This pond is north of Eckerman Corners (M-28 and M-123).  Drive North on M-123 for about 11 miles to Lakeshore Drive (Curley Lewis Highway) and turn right (East).  Continue on Lakeshore drive for about 7 miles to a paved scenic overlook parking lot.
sd: Fall fingerling brook trout are stocked annually here.  A 2014 survey found brook trout with an average length of about nine inches.  Fish ranged from 7 to 10 inches.
desig: Naomikong Pond is a designated Type A trout lake.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Lake Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing guide.

d: Pratt Lake is located within the Pretty Lakes Complex.  This complex of lakes is governed by a no motors restriction, both gas and electric motors are prohibited.  Most anglers fish this lake with Kayaks or float tubes.  Pratt Lake can be fished from shore in many places as the drop-off to deeper wa
dir: This location is North of the village of Newberry.  Drive North on M-123 to Four Mile Corner (County Road 407) and turn left.  Follow County Road (CR) 407 for about 17 miles to CR 416 and turn left (West).  Continue on CR 416 past Holland Lake and Pr
sd: Eagle Lake strain yearling rainbow trout are stocked annually.  These stocked rainbows survive very well and grow to lengths upwards of 17 inches.  Fish averaged a length of 13.5 inches during a 2014 survey.
desig: Pratt Lake is a designated Type A trout lake.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Lake regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

d: The entire stream is wadeable from the bridge upstream.  The stream flows through a hardwood forest making tag alder cover limited, but providing excellent instream woody debris for fish cover.  Anglers use spinners and natural bait on this stream.  Floating is not an option as there are too many bl

sd: Naturally reproducing populations of brook trout and steelhead are present here.  The steelhead run in the spring is light but offers some diversity to the fisheries in this area.  Brook trout range in size from six to ten inches, there are fewer lar
desig: Roxybury Creek is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in your current Michigan Fishing Guide.

d: This stretch of river offers opportunities to a variety of anglers.  Fly anglers get that "big water" feel on this stretch of river.  Spin casters using artificial lures or natural bait can fish floating, wading or from the bank.  The best access is coming in from the Lower Falls parking lot along t
dir: From Newberry, drive north on M-123 towards Paradise for about 26 miles to the Lower Falls Overlook entrance at Tahquamenon Falls State Park.  Use the viewing platform boardwalk to access the section of river just above the Lower Falls.  There is a h
sd: Yearling brown trout are stocked annually at the Upper Falls.  These fish will move their way down to the cascading stair-steps just above the Lower Falls where more groundwater is present with faster moving water.  Few anglers target this stretch of
desig: This section of the Tahquamenon River is not classified.  Please refer to the current Michigan Fishing Guide for regulations.

d: Wading is difficult making bank angling the primary approach here.  Anglers who want to float with a canoe or kayak can access the creek off of the CR 405 bridge.  Most anglers use natural bait and anglers who fish farther from the bridge tend to have better success.
dir: This site is located west of the village of Newberry.  Take Dollarville Road (County Road 405) west 3.3 miles to the bridge crossing on Teaspoon Creek.  From M-28, go north on County Road (CR) 405 for 1.3 miles to the bridge crossing.
sd: Yearling brook trout are stocked annually.  Anglers report good catches of brook trout with sizes ranging from 7 to 10 inches, not many bigger fish are reported.
desig: Teaspoon Creek at CR 405 is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: Two Hearted River Reed And Green Campground
d: Wading in the Two Hearted River is extremely challenging due to the nature of the dark water and deep holes.  There are plenty of bank angling opportunities at or near this site.  The campground is a popular put-in for kaykers and canoeists.  Typically steelhead can be caught here from early April t
dir: East of Deer Park, drive south on County Road (CR) 407 for about three miles to CR 410 and turn left (East).  Continue on CR 410 for about 4.5 miles looking for the sign for Reed and Green Campground.  Turn left onto CR 374 and follow the road for ab
sd: Yearling steelhead are stocked into the Two Hearted River annually and also naturally reproduce in this system.  The Two Hearted River is the premier river in the Eastern Upper Peninsula for steelhead fishing in the spring.  Fish of ten pounds or mor
desig: The Two Hearted River at the Reed and Green Campground is a designated Type 4 trout stream.  Refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: Two Hearted River at Rivermouth Campground
d: This site can be fished in a variety of different locations.  Starting on the beach, anglers can fish the entire bank of the river with no obstructions all the way downstream to Lake Superior.  Just upstream of the boating access site anglers can fish along the bank which skirts the dunes.  Anglers
dir: East of Deer Park, drive south on County Road (CR) 407 for about three miles to CR 410 and turn left (east).  Follow CR 410 for about seven miles until the road merges into CR 414.  Continue on CR 414 for about two miles to CR 423 and turn left (nort
sd: Yearling steelhead are stocked in the Two Hearted River annually and also reproduce naturally in this system.  The Two Hearted River is the premier river in the Eastern Upper Peninsula for steelhead fishing in the spring.  Fish of ten pounds or more
desig: The Two Hearted River at Rivermouth Campground is a designated Type 4 trout stream.  Refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

d: This is a tranquil small lake in central Luce County.  This is a perfect site for hand-carry watercraft or float tube anglers.  Most anglers will fish with deep running lures as the near shore habitat is limited for fish cover.  Some vegetation on the north end of the lake can serve as good fish cov
dir: This site is located north of the village of Newberry.  Drive north on M-123 for four miles to 4 Mile Corner (County Road 407) and turn left.  Follow County Road (CR) 407 for about 4.5 miles to CR 455 and turn left.  Follow CR 455 for about 8 miles t
sd: Fall fingerling brook trout are stocked annually and retired adult broodstock brook trout are stocked when available from the hatchery system.  Fish averaged about 9.5 inches during a 2016 Fisheries Division survey.  Fish generally range from six to
desig: Ward Lake is a designated Type A trout lake.  Refer to the Michigan Inland Trout and Salmon Lake Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: West Branch Teaspoon Creek County Road 363 Bridge
d: Wading is difficult making bank or boat angling the primary approach here.  Anglers who want to float with a canoe or kayak can access off of Foley Hill Road bridge or the CR 363 bridge.  Most anglers use natural bait and anglers that fish farthest from the bridge have better success.
dir: This site is southwest of the village of Newberry.  Follow M-123 south 3.5 miles to M-28 and turn right (west).  Follow M-28 for one mile to county (CR) 363 and turn right (north).  The second bridge crossing is a quarter mile from M-28 for access to
sd: Yearling brook trout are stocked annually.  Anglers report good catches of brook trout with sizes ranging from seven to ten inches.  Not many big fish have been reported.
desig: The West Branch Teaspon Creek ad the CR 363 bridge is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: Upper West Pine River At The Sand Trap
d: This location leads to a sediment basin and a spawning riffle built by the United States Forest Service (USFS).  Average stream widtch is 15 to 18 feet.  Depths are 1/2 to 3 ft.  The river is fairly shallow with pools on the meander bends.  Substrate is primarily sand and the sand can be deep in spo
dir: Take M-28 to Dick Road (USFS 3139) and turn left (south) on Dick Road to Lone Pine Road (USFS 3141).  Turn right (west) onto Lone Pine and go about 1.5 miles to USFS 3141 A.  This road leads to the river at the sediment basin and spawning riffle.
sd: Wild brook trout, juvenile steelhead (rainbow trout) and steelhead and salmon seasonally.
desig: The Upper West Pine River at the Sand Trap is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: Rifle River At Peters Road Crossing
d: This section of river is good from mid winter to fall.  It is easy to access for floating a canoe or kayak or for wading.  Any baits and lures may be used.
dir: Go south on M-33 from Rose City for six miles.  Turn left (east) on Peters road and proceed approximtely 3.5 miles to the Rifle River crossing.
sd: Brown trout are stocked at several locations in the Rifle River, and steelhead are stocked annually just downstream at Selkirk.  The river is managed here for the returning and larger trout and steelhead.
desig: The Rifle River at the Peters Road Crossing is a designated Type 3 trout stream.  Please refer to the Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: Rifle River at Ranch Road Bridge
d: The best fishing is immediately after the opener.  Baits to try include worms, spawn, spinners and flies.  The river can be accessed by canoe, kayak, wading or bank fishing as there is ample area in the Rifle River Recreation Area.
dir: From Rose City head east on Rose City road for five miles to the entrance of the Rifle River Recreation Area at Lupton.  Proceed south on main campground road towards the rustic camping sites (Ranch Road) until you get to the bridge over the river.
sd: Anglers may find brown trout of a variety of sizes (surveys showed up to 19 inches), and rainbow trout (steelhead) from smolts (7" to 2ft plus).  In the spring, the steelhead come up the river to spawn.  Brown trout are stocked at various locations a
desig: The Rifle River at the Ranch Road Bridge is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: Rifle River At Sage Lake Road
d: Sage Lake Bridge is the border deliniation point from a Type 1 stream to a Type 3 stream.  This area can be accessed via canoe, kayak or wading.  All baits can be used.
dir: From Rose City, take M-33 south for four miles to Sage Lake Road.  Turn left (east) and proceed about three and a half miles to the Sage Lake Road crossing.  There is an access and parking with a portable toiled and a place to launch on the north eas
sd: Brown trout are stocked at various locations in the Rifle River and rainbow trout (steelhead) are stocked annually at Selkirk.  Additionally some natural reproduction occurs and there is always a possibility of getting a brook trout which may drop do
desig: The Rifle River from Sage Lake Road to Saginaw Bay is a designateed Type 3 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Canoe Harbor Campground
d: There is cobble, gravel and sand holes in this area.  Extensive amounts of wood in the river make for good holding cover and a popular place to fish during the large mayfly night hatches.  Wading should be done carefully here.  This location is in the Mason Tract of State forest land.
dir: The access site may be reached from Grayling from the intersection of the I-75 Business Route and M-72, travel 9.2 miles east on M-72 and turn right on South Branch Road.  Drive 1.1 miles south, passing the campground on the left.  There is a small p
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are present here.  Brook trout up to ten inches are common and brown trout can grow to twenty inches or larger in this stretch.
desig: The South Branch of the Au Sable at Canoe Harbor Campground is a designated Gear Restricted Stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations of the current Michigan Fishing Guid

n: Black River At Black River State Forest Campground
d: The Black River supports a salmon spawning run and contains resident populations of both brook and rainbow trout.

sd: A Fisheries Survey conducted in 2013 reported catches of brook trout, Chinook salmon, rainbow trout and coho salmon.

d: Cobble, gravel and sand substrate are all present here.  This is a popular location to fish and is mostly for kayaks, canoes and drift boats to begin the trip into the Mason Tract. sd: Wild brook and brown trout are present here.  Brook trout up to ten inches are common and brown trout can grow to 18 inches or larger in this reach.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Deerheart Valley Road Ending
d: This reach is more popular with kayakers and canoers.  Anglers should be aware of the private land in this area.
dir: Take M-18 northeast out of Roscommon to Deerheart Valley road and turn left (north).  Deerheart Valley Road will end at the river, parking is limited.
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to eight inches are present but not common.  Brown trout can grow up to 18 inches or larger in this reach but are not abundant.  Both species likely use this reach of river more during
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at Deerheart Valley Road Ending is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Gui

n: South Branch Au Sable River Dogtown
d: Cobble, gravel and sand holes are all present in this stretch.  There is some wood in the river although this reach is generally wider and shallower than other areas.  This is the reach of river where historic trout population estimates were made with electrofishing gear from the 1970's through the
dir: From Grayling, take M-72 east to South Branch Road and turn right (south).  Drive south/southwest past Canoe Harbor Campground and continue for about a total of 1.6 miles where you will find a parking area along the road for about ten vehicles.
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are present.  Brook trout up to ten inches are commong and brown trout can grow to twenty inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at Dogtown is a designated Gear Restricted Stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at Downeys Stone Wall is a designated Gear Restricted Stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: East Branch Of Fox River At M-77 Bridge
d: The Fox River is noted for its wild brook trout fishery. This location contains deep holes, undercut banks and woody debris - all of which are characteristics of good trout water.
dir: From Seney, travel north 4.1 miles on M-77 to reach the M-77 Bridge. You can access the river by using the right-of-way along the road and bridge.
sd: This section of river contains fish in a range of sizes from three to 20 inches in total length (both brook and brown trout).
desig: This is a Type 1 trout stream. Please review the Inland Trout and Salmon Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Durants Castle
d: Cobble, gravel and sand substrate are all present here.  This river reach is generally lower gradient than downstream areas.  From turnaround parking log, anglers must walk about 300 yards to the river through a well marked path.  A historic house (castle) was once located on this site.  This is in
dir: From Chase Bridge, go .2 miles north on Chase Bridge Road and turn right.  Continue on this road 2.5 miles and turn right.  The parking area is .1 miles from the turn.  There is a .15 mile path that leads down a steep slope to the river.  From the pa
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to ten inches are common and brown trout can grow to twenty inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The south Branch Au Sable River at Durants Castle is a designated Gear Restricted stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: Fox River At Fox River State Forest Campground
d: The Fox River is noted for its wild brook trout fishery and it is managed as a "Natural River".

sd: Fisheries survey data report brook trout in a range in size from 1 to 19 inches in total length.

d: The Fox River is noted for its wild brook trout fishery and is managed as a "Natural River".
dir: From the intersection of M-28 and M-77 in Seney, travel west on M-28 for approximately 500 feet. Access the river by using the right-of-way along the road and bridge.
sd: Fisheries survey data reports a range of brook trout in sizes from 1 to 19 inches in total length.
desig: The area of the Fox River at M-28 has split regulation types. Upstream from the M-28 bridge to the Fox River State Forest Campground, the Fox River is a designated a Gear Restricted (GR) trout stream.

n: Fox River At Seney Municipal State Forest Campground
d: The Fox River is noted for its wild brook trout fishery and is managed as a "Natural River".
dir: From the intersection of M-28 and M-77 in Seney, travel west on M-28 for approximately .3 miles and turn right onto School Street (will turn into Fox River Road). Travel on School Street (Fox River Road) for approximately .7 miles. The Seney Municipa
sd: Fisheries survey data reports a range of brook trout sizes from 1 to 19 inches in total length.
desig: This is a Gear Restricted stream. Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Durants Hangar
d: Cobble, gravel and sand substrate are all found here.  Large sandy turnaround for about four vehicles after traversing a long very narrow two-track is available.  A moderate walk takes the angler to the river.  This is in the Mason Tract of State forest land.
dir: From Chase Bridge, go .2 miles north on Chase Bridge Road.  Turn right on the gravel road and turn right again after 2.7 miles.  This turn-off is .3 miles past the road entering from the west.  Follow the road as it curves to the left after .1 miles.
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to ten inches are common and brown trout can grow to twenty inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at Durants Hangar is a designated Gear Restricted Stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Forest Rest
d: Cobble, gravel and sand substrate are all present in this area.  A moderate walk of about 5 to 10 minutes from the parking lot takes the angler to the river through a lowland spruce and cedar swamp.  This is in the Mason Tract of State forest land.
dir: From Chase Bridge head south to Hickey Creek Trail, then east to leline Road.  Take Leline road two-track trail left to the turnaround and walk down to the river along an unimproved path, veering away from private cabin.  Anglers should be aware of p
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to ten inches are common and brown trout can grow to twenty inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch of the Au Sable River at Forest Rest is a designated Gear Restricted stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River High Banks
d: Cobble, gravel and sand substrate are all present here.  The parking lot offers room for multiple vehicles but it is sandy at times and narrow.  This is a very scenic area that offers the angler a more remote feel.  This is in the Mason Tract of State forest land.
dir: From Chase Bridge:  Go .2 miles north on Chase Bridge Road and turn right.  Continue for about 2.8 miles and turn right again.  Drive through the parking area for Durant's Castle and take the first road to the right (approximately 1/2 mile from the t
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to ten inches are common and brown trout ucan grow to twenty inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch of the Au Sable River @ the High Banks is a designated Gear Restricted Stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Lelands Lot
d: Cobble, gravel and sand bottom are all present here.  The Mason Tract Pathway parallels the river in this area and provides multiple access points.  The parking lot offers room for two to three vehicles and this site is in the Mason Tract of State forest land.
dir: Heading east on M-72 from Grayling, turn right (south) onto South Branch Road.  Continue on South Branch Road past Canoe Harbor Campground for a total of about 2.2 miles to the parking area on the left side of the road.
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to ten inches are common and brown trout can grow to twenty inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The south Branch Au Sable River at Lelands Lot is a designated Gear Restricted Stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Lower High Banks
d: This site has cobble, gravel and sand substrate.  The parking lot offers room for multiple vehicles but it is sandy at times and narrow.  This is a very scenic area that offers the angler a more remote feel.  It is located in the Mason Tract of State forest land.
dir: Head east on M-72 from Grayling and turn right (south) on South Branch Road.  Continue on South Branch Road passing by Canoe Harbor Campground for a total of 4.1 miles.  Bear left at the fork in the road and continue on the two-track for about a half
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to ten inches are common and brown trout grow to twenty inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at the Lower High Banks is a designated Gear Restricted Stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Meads Landing
d: This reach is more popular with kayakers and canoers.  Anglers should be aware of the private land in the area.
dir: From M-18 highway north of the town of Roscommon, take Steckert Road north a short distance out of town to the public access signs.  Parking is ample in this location.

desig: The South Branch of the Au Sable River at Meads Landing is a designated as a Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Potters Landing
d: Cobble, gravel and sand substrate are present here.  This is a popular location to fish.  A walk of less than 5 minutes from the parking lot takes you to the stairs along the river.  This is in the Mason Tract of State forest land and many anglers will take the paralleling Mason Tract pathway to fis
dir: From Roscommon, take M-18 to Chase Bridge Road and turn left (north).  Continue on Chase Bridge Road for about 2.3 miles until you cross the bridge over the river, turn right on the gravel road (Mason Tract Trail) after the bridge and then turn right
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to ten inches are common and brown trout can grow to twenty inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at Potters Landing is a designated Gear Restricted stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Smith Bridge

dir: From the M-72 bridge crossing of the South Branch Au Sable River, take M-72 west about a quarter mile and follow the signs on the north side of the road back to the parking lot at Smith Bridge.  This site is about 13 miles east of I-75 off of M-72.
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to 9 inches are common and brown trout can grow to 20 inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at Smith Bridge is a designated Gear Restricted stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Steckert Bridge Road Access
d: This reach is more popular with kayakers and canoers.  Anglers should be aware of the private land in this area.
dir: From Roscommon, take M-18 to Steckert Bridge Road (Lancewood in town) and turn left (north).  Continue on Steckert Bridge Road for about 2 miles to the access site.
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present.  Brook trout up to 8 inches can be found but are not common.  Brown trout can grow to 18 inches or larger in this reach but are not very abundant.  Both species likely use this reach of river more during s
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at Steckert Bridge Road Access is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guid

n: South Branch Au Sable River Swamp

dir: From Roscommon, take M-18 north/northeast to Chase Bridge Road and turn left (north).  Continue on Chase Bridge road for about 2.3 miles to Mason Tract Trail (gravel road) and turn right (east).  Continue on the Mason Tract Trail for about 1.8 miles
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to 10 inches are common and brown trout grow to 20 inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at The Swamp is a designated Gear Restricted Stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Truettner Tract
d: Cobble, gravel and sand holes are all present here.  There is also a fair amount of wood in the stream.  Wading is generally pretty easy but there are some deep holes to be aware of.  This is on State land but anglers should be aware of some private property.
dir: From the Smith Bridge/M-72 crossing, head west and take the second two track to the north to the parking lot.  Use the stairs to access the river.  This site is just over 12 miles to the east of I-75 off of M-72.
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to 9 inches are common and brown trout can grow to 20 inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River at the Truettner Tract is a designated Gear Restricted Stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: South Branch Au Sable River Upper Baldwins
d: Cobble, gravel and sand substrate are all present here.  The parking area offers room for multiple vehicles but is very sandy.  The long and narrow two-track into the parking turnaround will likely scratch your vehicle and you will have to walk a considerable distance behind the gate to get to the r
dir: From M-72 turn south on South Branch Road.  Continue on South Branch Road past Canoe Harbor Campground for a total of 3.2 miles.  Turn left on the road leading to Upper and Lower Baldwin's.  Continue a little over half a mile on this two track and yo
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are both present here.  Brook trout up to 10 inches are common and brown trout can grow to 20 inches or larger in this reach.
desig: The South Branch Au Sable River Upper Baldwins is a designated Gear Restricted stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: North Branch Au Sable River At County Line
d: The river in this reach is approximately 100 feet wide and is easily wadeable.
dir: Take Old State Road east from Gaylord to Crapo Lake Road.  Turn right (south) and continue to the first two-track on the west (right) side just beyond the county line.  The parking area is at the end of the two-track.
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are found here.
desig: The North Branch Au Sable River at the County Line is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: North Branch Au Sable River At The Black Hole
d: This area of the river was dredged historically, wade carefully.
dir: From County Road (CR) 612, turn north on Twin Bridges road and drive for just over three miles and turn left.  Continue on this lane for about 75 yards to a large parking area.  You will find a stairway that leads to the river.
sd: Wild brook and brown trout are found here.
desig: The North Branch Au Sable River at the Black Hole is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

sl: Brook Trout, Brown Trout, Lake Trout, Rainbow Trout, Splake
d: This lake provides a nice hook-and-bobber nearshore fishery for panfish species, as well as a mid-water fishery for trout. Bait can be purchased at Buck's sporting goods store in downtown Ishpeming.
dir: Take Highway US-41 to Marquette County's town of Ishpeming. At Lakeshore Drive and Highway US-41 (in the vicinity of the traffic light just west of the McDonald's, and at the corner of Jubilee Foods grocery store), turn south onto Lakeshore Drive. Pr
sd: This lake is stocked regularly with brook, brown, lake and rainbow trout and splake.
desig: This lake is a Type C lake. Please see the Inland Trout & Salmon Regulations within the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

n: North Branch Au Sable River At The Sand Trap

sd: Wild brook and brown trout can be found here.
desig: The North Branch of the Au Sable River at the Sand Trap is a designated Type 1 trout stream.  Please refer to the Inland Trout and Salmon Stream Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

dir: This waterbody is located 9 miles south of L'Anse, Michigan on State Highway US-41.

sd: The Otter River is a 20-foot wide, knee deep, coldwater trout stream that is ideal for both fly or spincast fishing. Resident fish here are wild brook trout, supplemented periodically with stockings of Jumbo River-strain brook trout (parentage from O
desig: This is a Type 1 trout stream. Please refer to the Inland Trout & Salmon Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

dir: On State Highway M-28 at the town of Kenton, at a distance of .2 miles east of Kenton, take Forest Highway 16 north for .2 miles to Sparrow Rapids Road on your left (west). Follow this road for 4.2 miles until it ends, then walk 1/4 mile down a dirt
sd: Trout species found here include wild and stocked brook trout, brown trout and steelhead.
desig: This is a Type 3 river. Please refer to the Inland Trout & Salmon Regulations in the current Michigan Fishing Guide.

d: The river is easy to wade and offers open tree canopy that is favorable for fly fishing enthusiasts. There are no bait or tackle shops in Wakefield, Bessemer or Ironwood - other than purchasing worms and crawlers from any Holiday gas station location.
dir: From the town of Bessemer, proceed north at the intersection of Highway US-2 and North Moore Street. In .7 miles, North Moore Street turns left and becomes Black River Road. Continue north on Black River Road for 2 miles until you come to Hedburg Roa

sd: Wild brook trout to 10 inches and wild brown trout to 20 inches can be found here.

sd: Wild brook trout to 12 inches and wild brown trout to 20 inches plus can be found here.

sd: Wild brook trout to 12 inches and wild brown to 20 inches or larger can be found here.

sd: Wild brook trout to 12 inches and wild brown trout to 20 inches plus can be found here.

d: Trueman Lake is a 6 acre lake located within the Hiawatha National Forest.  Hike Lake has been managed for Brook Trout for more than 60 years.  Trueman Lake is a Type A Trout Lake which provides anglers trout fishing opportunities from the last Saturday in April to September 30th.

sd: Stocked with fall fingerling Assinica strain Brook Trout.
